calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2020 Continued To Apr-08-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Elizabeth O'Brien, Esq. of GORDON RESS SCULLY MANSUKHANI LLP, T:916.565.2900, for defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court and counsel discussed plaintiff's claim that he has filed a notice of unavailability. No such document appears on the register of action. The Court also denies defendant's counsel's oral request to declare plaintiff a vexatious litigant. Therefore, there being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BRAM FRIDHANDLER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is adopted, as follows:

Defendant Bram Fridhandler's demurrer to plaintiff Abhijit Prasad's complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action as the doctrine of res judicata or claim preclusion bars his claims. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.10(e).) "Res judicata bars a cause of action that was or could have been litigated in a prior proceeding if: '(1) the present action is on the same cause of action as the prior proceeding; (2) the prior proceeding resulted in a final judgment on the merits; and (3) the parties in the present action or parties in privity with them were parties to the prior proceeding. [Citation.]'" (Federal Home Loan Bank of San Francisco v. Countrywide Financial Corp. (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 1520, 1527.) In this case, all of the elements for the application of the doctrine of claim preclusion are present.

The court grants defendant's request for judicial notice per Evidence Code 452(d). The court is allowed to take judicial notice of court records. Those records disclose that plaintiff commenced case CGC-18-566564 against defendant based on the same facts that underpin the instant action. Judge Kahn dismissed the prior action based on the doctrine of quasi-judicial immunity and judgment was subsequently entered on March 18, 2019. Plaintiff did not appeal that judgment and it is final. The judgment was on the merits as the court determined "the real or substantial grounds of action or defense as distinguished from matters of practice, procedure, jurisdiction or form." (Boccardo v. Safeway Stores, Inc. (1982) 134 Cal.App.3d 1037, 1042.)

The court rejects plaintiff's argument that Judge Kahn did not consider his contractual claims. The prior complaint clearly alleges a claim for breach of contract. (CGC-18-566564 Complaint, 5.6.1 - 5.6.15.) Judge Kahn's order sustaining the demurrer did so as to "ALL CAUSES OF ACTION," which necessarily includes the breach of contract claim. If plaintiff believed that the doctrine of quasi-judicial immunity did not apply to a breach of contract claim, then it was incumbent upon plaintiff to appeal Judge Kahn's decision rather than commence repetitive litigation.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
